_note: Ready-to-insert interactive_image block for a nephron (with blood supply). Image: nephron_illus.webp (keyed transparent + compressed from the founder's generated PNG, 880x880 square). Founder verified this diagram against standard references (canonical layout: single clean loop of Henle, straight collecting duct — the earlier 3D attempt was rejected as non-standard). To ship: upload the webp to R2 via the books asset-upload route, put the URL in `src`, add this block to the target biology page (Class 11 Excretory Products / kidney). Hotspots authored + browser-verified against the image on 2026-07-13.
alt: Structure of a nephron with its blood supply
caption: 📸 Tap each dot to explore — or hit 'Test yourself' to play Label Sprint

hotspots.label: Glomerulus
hotspots.detail: A tuft of capillaries where blood is **filtered** under high pressure; sits inside Bowman's capsule.

hotspots.label: Bowman's capsule
hotspots.detail: The double-walled cup that surrounds the glomerulus and **collects the filtrate**. Together they form the **Malpighian/renal corpuscle**.

hotspots.label: Afferent arteriole
hotspots.detail: Brings blood **into** the glomerulus. It is **wider** than the efferent arteriole, which keeps filtration pressure high.

hotspots.label: Proximal convoluted tubule
hotspots.detail: The first coiled tubule after the capsule. **Most reabsorption** happens here (glucose, amino acids, most water and salts).

hotspots.label: Distal convoluted tubule
hotspots.detail: The coiled tubule before the collecting duct; fine-tunes **salt and water** balance (under ADH/aldosterone).

hotspots.label: Loop of Henle
hotspots.detail: The hairpin loop (descending + ascending limbs) that sets up the **concentration gradient** in the medulla to conserve water.

hotspots.label: Collecting duct
hotspots.detail: Carries urine from several nephrons toward the pelvis; **final water reabsorption** happens here under ADH.

hotspots.label: Peritubular capillaries
hotspots.detail: The capillary network wrapping the convoluted tubules that **reabsorbs** the useful substances back into the blood.

hotspots.label: Vasa recta
hotspots.detail: The straight capillaries running alongside the loop of Henle that **maintain** the medullary gradient.

generation_prompt: A clean anatomically accurate educational illustration of a single human nephron with its blood supply, in the standard biology-textbook schematic layout, semi-realistic painterly style. Bowman's capsule + glomerulus top-left; afferent (thicker) + efferent arterioles; proximal convoluted tubule coiling near top-left; a single narrow loop of Henle descending, hairpin U-turn, ascending; distal convoluted tubule top-right; one straight collecting duct down the right (not a branching tree); peritubular capillaries around the tubules and straight vasa recta beside the loop, red arterial / blue venous. Muted palette, flat, solid deep-charcoal background #121316, no text/labels/numbers/leader lines. Portrait/square, high resolution.
